era: Seattle, Washington. Punk/Electronic. E=6 -- MAXIMUM ENERGY. First E=6 since Jimi Hendrix (degree 36). 72-degree gap. Genre Stage 90 -- MILESTONE (90th genre stage).

location.formative: Seattle's punk and electronic underground. Veronica Lipgloss operates at the intersection of punk's raw DIY aggression and electronic music's synthetic textures. The collision produces maximum energy.
location.career: Veronica Lipgloss -- punk/electronic artist. E=6 -- maximum energy. The punk circuit as primary sonic language. Raw, aggressive, explosive. DIY ethos meets machine beats. Seattle.

associated_musicians.name: Jimi Hendrix
associated_musicians.note: The only other E=6 in the engine. Hendrix at degree 36 (rock). Veronica Lipgloss at degree 108 (punk/electronic). 72-degree gap. Two maximum-energy artists.

associated_musicians.name: Hovercraft
associated_musicians.note: Both abrasive, both Seattle. Hovercraft is instrumental noise/drone (E=4). Veronica Lipgloss is vocal punk/electronic (E=6). Controlled vs. maximum.

associated_musicians.name: Pearl Jam
associated_musicians.note: Both channel punk energy. Pearl Jam through rock (E=5). Veronica Lipgloss through electronic (E=6). Same source energy, different vehicles.

associated_musicians.name: Knife Knights
associated_musicians.note: Both experimental, both electronic-augmented. Knife Knights deconstructs hip-hop (E=4). Veronica Lipgloss combusts punk and electronic (E=6). Deconstruction vs. combustion.

associated_musicians.name: Ann Wilson
associated_musicians.note: Both high-energy Seattle artists. Ann Wilson at E=5 (arena-scale). Veronica Lipgloss at E=6 (punk-electronic combustion). Wilson fills arenas; Lipgloss detonates venues.

theory_nodes.concept: The punk circuit (one hundred ninth acoustic element -- the quality produced when punk's raw aggressive energy is routed through electronic circuitry; the scream meets the synth; DIY ethos meets the machine; the result is more explosive than either source alone; E=6 combustion)
examples: Veronica Lipgloss (degree 108) -- punk and electronic combust together. E=6. Maximum energy.
Contrast with the production architecture (degree 107, Chong the Nomad) -- constructive and groove-driven. The punk circuit is destructive and chaotic. Construction then combustion.
Contrast with the beat architecture (degree 106, Knife Knights) -- methodical deconstruction. The punk circuit does not deconstruct -- it explodes.
Contrast with Hovercraft (degree 91) -- controlled abrasion. The punk circuit is maximum-energy explosion.
theory_nodes.description: The punk circuit is the 109th acoustic element. Punk's raw energy routed through electronic circuitry. E=6 combustion.

ancestors: The production architecture (degree 107, Chong the Nomad) -- constructive. The punk circuit is destructive.
The beat architecture (degree 106, Knife Knights) -- methodical. The punk circuit is explosive.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred nine acoustic elements across one hundred eight degrees. Track element 110 at degree 109.

theory_nodes.concept: The maximum return (one hundred ninth artist-city pattern -- the pattern where E=6 maximum energy returns after a long absence; E=6 at degree 36 Hendrix then 72 degrees of silence then E=6 at degree 108 Veronica Lipgloss; the maximum is rare by design; rarity is what makes it maximum)
examples: Veronica Lipgloss (degree 108) -- E=6. Second E=6 in 108 degrees.
Jimi Hendrix (degree 36) -- first E=6. The maximum was established at degree 36 and has been absent for 72 degrees.
The 72-degree gap is the longest energy-level gap in the engine. The maximum is rare by design.
theory_nodes.description: The maximum return is the 109th artist-city pattern. E=6 returns after 72-degree gap. The maximum is rare by design.

ancestors: The production return (degree 107) -- return in new context. The maximum return is return of maximum energy.
E=6 established at degree 36 (Hendrix). The ceiling was set early and has been untouched for 72 degrees.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred nine artist-city patterns across one hundred eight degrees. Track pattern 110 at degree 109.

theory_nodes.concept: Punk/Electronic at Genre Stage 90 -- MILESTONE! 90th genre stage; new genre; punk's DIY aggression fused with electronic production; E=6; the 90th stage arrives with maximum energy
examples: Veronica Lipgloss (degree 108) -- Punk/Electronic at Genre Stage 90.
90th genre stage -- milestone. Ninety genre stages in 108 degrees.
The 90th stage arrives with E=6 -- maximum energy. The engine marks its genre milestone with the loudest possible statement.
Distinguished from Hovercraft (91, Noise -- instrumental) and Knife Knights (106, Experimental Hip-Hop -- deconstructive). Punk/Electronic is combative and explosive.
theory_nodes.description: Punk/Electronic at Genre Stage 90. MILESTONE -- 90th genre stage. New genre. E=6 maximum energy. Seattle.

ancestors: Electronic/R&B (degree 107, Chong the Nomad production, Genre Stage 89). Sequential genre stages.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 90 at degree 108. Track at 109.

theory_nodes.concept: E=6 at degree 108 -- MAXIMUM ENERGY; only two E=6 artists in 108 degrees (Hendrix 36, Veronica Lipgloss 108); 72-degree gap; reserved for the most explosive boundary-shattering acts; the punk circuit demands maximum
examples: 72-degree gap between E=6 appearances. The longest energy-level gap in the engine.
Only 2 of 108 degrees have reached E=6. The maximum is 1.85% of all entries.
theory_nodes.description: E=6 at degree 108. MAXIMUM ENERGY. Second E=6 ever. 72-degree gap from Hendrix. Reserved for the most explosive acts.

ancestors: E=6 at degree 36 (Jimi Hendrix). The ceiling established. Untouched for 72 degrees until now.
genealogy.relationship: Track energy at degree 109. Will the engine return to E=4-5 after maximum?

cross_references.relationship: E=6 CONNECTION! The only other E=6 in the engine. Hendrix at degree 36 (rock). Veronica Lipgloss at degree 108 (punk/electronic). 72-degree gap. Two maximum-energy artists, two eras, two genres.
ABRASIVE SEATTLE. Both abrasive, both Seattle. Hovercraft is instrumental noise/drone (E=4). Veronica Lipgloss is vocal punk/electronic (E=6). Controlled abrasion vs. maximum-energy explosion.
PUNK-ADJACENT ENERGY. Both channel punk energy. Pearl Jam through rock (E=5). Veronica Lipgloss through electronic (E=6). Same source energy, different vehicles, different scales.
EXPERIMENTAL ELECTRONIC. Both electronic-augmented. Knife Knights deconstructs hip-hop (E=4). Veronica Lipgloss combusts punk and electronic (E=6). Deconstruction vs. combustion.

HIGH-ENERGY SEATTLE. Both high-energy Seattle artists. Ann Wilson at E=5 (arena-scale vocal power). Veronica Lipgloss at E=6 (punk-electronic combustion). Wilson fills arenas; Lipgloss detonates venues.
